title: Full-text description search performs full table scan after v2.3 upgrade (~12s for 5,000 tasks)
problem: Since upgrading from TaskFlow v2.2 to v2.3, searching across task descriptions takes 10-15 seconds consistently. Title-only search remains fast. The user has approximately 5,000 tasks, many with long descriptions containing pasted meeting notes. The slowdown is CPU-bound (laptop fan spins up) and shows no caching improvement on repeated queries.
root_cause_hypothesis: The v2.3 update likely removed, broke, or replaced the full-text index on the task descriptions column — or switched the description search implementation from an indexed full-text query to a naive unindexed scan (e.g., LIKE '%term%' or in-memory regex). Evidence: (1) title search is still fast (its index was preserved), (2) search time is constant regardless of how many results match (points to scanning all rows, not result processing), (3) CPU spike indicates row-by-row computation rather than index lookup, (4) no caching benefit rules out a warm-cache optimization path.
reproduction_steps: Install TaskFlow v2.3 (or upgrade from v2.2 to v2.3)
Have a dataset with ~5,000 tasks, some with long description fields
Perform a description search for any term (common or rare)
Observe ~10-15 second response time and high CPU utilization
Compare with a title-only search on the same dataset — title search returns quickly
environment: TaskFlow v2.3 (upgraded from v2.2), ~5,000 tasks with some containing long descriptions (pasted meeting notes), running on a work laptop (specific OS and DB engine not confirmed)
impact: Any user with a non-trivial number of tasks (~thousands) who searches by description will experience 10-15 second delays. Title-only search still works as a partial workaround. The feature is usable but painfully slow, degrading daily workflow for power users.
recommended_fix: 1. Diff the search query/implementation between v2.2 and v2.3 — look for changes to how description search is executed (check for dropped indexes, removed FTS configuration, or switch from indexed to LIKE/regex scan). 2. Verify the full-text index on the descriptions column exists and is being used (run EXPLAIN/query plan). 3. If the index was dropped, restore it. If the implementation changed, revert the description search path to the indexed approach. 4. Test with a dataset of 5,000+ tasks with large description fields to confirm sub-second response times are restored.
proposed_test_case: Create a test dataset with 5,000 tasks where at least 100 have descriptions >500 characters. Run a description search for a term appearing in only 2 tasks and a term appearing in 500 tasks. Assert both return in under 2 seconds. Run the same test with title search to confirm parity. Verify via query plan that the description search uses an index, not a sequential scan.
information_gaps: Exact database backend (SQLite, PostgreSQL, etc.) — though the fix direction is the same regardless
Whether v2.3 release notes or changelog mention search changes
Exact OS and hardware specs of the reporter's laptop
